messages.content: Tell me — did AirLLM run on the Apple Silicon machine in airbench?
No. AirLLM is CUDA and MLX centric and its import chain failed on Apple Silicon, so airbench used an equivalent layer-streaming demo instead — a documented constraint.
Question: Did AirLLM run on the Apple Silicon machine in airbench?
No. AirLLM is CUDA and MLX centric and its import chain failed on Apple Silicon, so airbench used an equivalent layer-streaming demo instead — a documented constraint.
QLoRA fine-tunes small LoRA adapters on top of a 4-bit quantized base model, so even an 8 GB MacBook that cannot run a 7B for inference can still fine-tune an LLM.
Briefly: What is QLoRA in airbench?
QLoRA fine-tunes small LoRA adapters on top of a 4-bit quantized base model, so even an 8 GB MacBook that cannot run a 7B for inference can still fine-tune an LLM.
